key_points: Delhi first became the capital of a kingdom under the Tomara Rajputs.
The Delhi Sultans built many cities in the area that we now know as Delhi, such as Dehli-i-Kuhna, Siri, and Jahanpanah.
History of this period is found in 'Tawarikh' (plural of Tarikh), which were written in Persian, the official language of the administration.
Raziyya, the daughter of Sultan Iltutmish, became the first female Sultan of Delhi in 1236.
The five dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate were: Early Turkish Rulers, Khalji, Tughluq, Sayyid, and Lodi.
examples: Qutb Minar: A famous monument started by Qutbuddin Aybak and finished by Iltutmish.
Garrison Towns: Fortified settlements where soldiers lived to protect the kingdom.
Iqta: A piece of land given to military commanders (Iqtadars) instead of a cash salary.
The Mongols: Warriors from Central Asia, led by Genghis Khan, who frequently attacked the Sultanate.
formulas: Tawarikh = Historical records written by learned men (Secretaries, Poets, Administrators)
exam_tips: Remember that Raziyya was removed from the throne in 1240 because the nobles did not want to be ruled by a woman.
Note that the Sultanate was strongest during the reigns of Ghiyasuddin Balban, Alauddin Khalji, and Muhammad Tughluq.
Identify that 'Hinterland' refers to the lands adjacent to a city or port that supply it with goods.
Understand that the 'Bandagan' were special slaves purchased for military service and favored by the Sultans.
